{"key_event":"Xi-era centralization and digital securitization","reasoning":"From an already very high 2000s state-power baseline, Xi-era centralization, anti-corruption discipline, Party penetration of institutions and firms, digital surveillance, NGO/legal crackdowns, and Xinjiang securitization pushed state capacity closer to total social penetration, despite economic and demographic headwinds. Civil society retained pockets of mobilization, especially online and in Hong Kong, but autonomous organization, rights lawyering, media, religion, academia, and NGOs were substantially narrowed, leaving society weaker than in the more pluralized 2000s.","society_change":-1.0,"society_power":3.3,"state_change":0.5,"state_power":9.7}
